article_title: Japan's GDP Growth Surpasses Expectations in Q4 2025
summary: Analyzed 5 factual claim(s) from the article. 2 claim(s) were verified by multiple sources. 1 claim(s) are likely true based on available evidence. 1 claim(s) have conflicting evidence. 1 claim(s) could not be verified.
concerns: Claim #4 (DISPUTED): Japan's semiconductor exports grew 45% year-over-year in 2025

claims.original_sentence: The Japanese economy posted a 2.1% growth rate in the fourth quarter of 2025, according to preliminary government data.
claims.explanation: Multiple official sources including the Cabinet Office confirm Japan's Q4 2025 GDP growth at 2.1%, matching the article's claim exactly.

sources.title: Japan GDP Growth Rate - Cabinet Office
Japan economy grows faster than expected - Reuters
sources.snippet: Japan's GDP expanded 2.1% in Q4 2025, beating analyst forecasts of 1.8%

claims.text: The Bank of Japan maintained its interest rate at 0.5% throughout 2025
claims.original_sentence: The central bank held rates steady at 0.5% for the entire year, providing a stable monetary environment.
claims.explanation: BOJ meeting minutes and official statements confirm the policy rate was maintained at 0.5% throughout 2025.

sources.title: BOJ Monetary Policy Meetings 2025 - Bank of Japan
sources.snippet: Policy rate decisions for 2025: maintained at 0.50%

claims.text: Consumer spending in Japan increased by 3.2% in the second half of 2025
claims.original_sentence: Robust consumer spending, up 3.2% in H2, drove much of the growth.
claims.explanation: Government statistics show consumer spending growth in H2 2025, though exact figures vary between 2.9% and 3.4% depending on the measure used.

sources.title: Household Spending Survey - Statistics Bureau
sources.snippet: Household spending rose approximately 3% in the latter half of 2025

claims.text: Japan's semiconductor exports grew 45% year-over-year in 2025
claims.original_sentence: The semiconductor sector was a particular bright spot, with exports surging 45% compared to the previous year.
claims.explanation: While semiconductor exports did grow significantly, the 45% figure is higher than most official trade statistics suggest. JETRO data indicates growth closer to 28-32%.

sources.title: Japan Trade Statistics - JETRO
sources.snippet: Semiconductor and related equipment exports grew approximately 30% in 2025

sources.title: Asia Semiconductor Market Report 2025
sources.snippet: Japan semiconductor exports showed strong growth of 28-35% depending on category

contradictions: JETRO reports 30% growth vs article's claim of 45%

claims.text: Toyota announced plans to invest 5 trillion yen in EV production by 2030
claims.original_sentence: Major corporate investments, including Toyota's 5 trillion yen EV commitment through 2030, signal continued confidence.
claims.explanation: While Toyota has announced significant EV investments, the specific 5 trillion yen figure and 2030 timeline could not be confirmed from recent official announcements.

sources.title: Toyota EV Strategy Update
sources.snippet: Toyota announced multi-trillion yen investment plans for electrification
